title: Early stage BC treatment
body:
I have not yet received a BC diagnosis; I am a young BRCA patient with a 70% chance of developing breast cancer in my lifetime. I am weighing my options re: monitoring (twice annual MRIs) vs prophylactic mastectomy. Research shows that there is no survival benefit to BRCA patients getting mastectomies (monitoring every 6 months is just as protective mortality-wise, since cancer is caught early with the 6 month MRI regimen). However, as this group knows better than anyone, chemo and radiation seem really, really shitty (I am so sorry to those in this community currently going through it). For those who have been diagnosed with stage 0 or stage 1 BC, would you mind sharing the stage and treatment you required? Has anyone been able to go straight to surgery without chemo/radiation?

Thank you in advance, sending love to all of you
